texto: The abuser does not believe, however, that his level of authority over the children should be in any way connected to his actual level of effort or sacrifice on their behalf, or to how much knowledge he actually has about who they are or what is going on in their lives. He considers it his right to make the ultimate determination of what is good for them even if he doesn’t attend to their needs or even if he only contributes to those aspects of child care that he enjoys or that make him look like a great dad in public.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lundy Bancroft Bancroft: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “The abuser does not believe, however, that his level of authority over the children should be in …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, abuse, abusive-men pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e abusive-men.
